children.heading: Watershed management of wet weather discharges
children.label: 1 Watershed management of wet weather discharges
children.text: The management of municipal combined sewer overflows, sanitary sewer overflows, and stormwater discharges, on an integrated watershed or subwatershed basis for the purpose of demonstrating the effectiveness of a unified wet weather approach.

children.heading: Stormwater best management practices
children.label: 2 Stormwater best management practices
children.text: The control of pollutants from municipal separate storm sewer systems for the purpose of demonstrating and determining controls that are cost-effective and that use innovative technologies to manage, reduce, treat, recapture, or reuse municipal stormwater, including techniques that utilize infiltration, evapotranspiration, and reuse of stormwater onsite.

children.heading: Watershed partnerships
children.label: 3 Watershed partnerships
children.text: Efforts of municipalities and property owners to demonstrate cooperative ways to address nonpoint sources of pollution to reduce adverse impacts on water quality.

children.heading: Integrated water resource plan
children.label: 4 Integrated water resource plan
children.text: The development of an integrated water resource plan for the coordinated management and protection of surface water, ground water, and stormwater resources on a watershed or subwatershed basis to meet the objectives, goals, and policies of this chapter.

children.heading: Municipality-wide stormwater management planning
children.label: 5 Municipality-wide stormwater management planning
children.text: The development of a municipality-wide plan that identifies the most effective placement of stormwater technologies and management approaches, to reduce water quality impairments from stormwater on a municipality-wide basis.

children.heading: Increased resilience of treatment works
children.label: 6 Increased resilience of treatment works
children.text: Efforts to assess future risks and vulnerabilities of publicly owned treatment works to manmade or natural disasters, including extreme weather events and sea-level rise, and to carry out measures, on a systemwide or area-wide basis, to increase the resiliency of publicly owned treatment works.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: b Administration
children.text: The Administrator, in coordination with the States, shall provide municipalities participating in a pilot project under this section the ability to engage in innovative practices, including the ability to unify separate wet weather control efforts under a single permit.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: c Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than , the Administrator shall transmit to Congress a report on the results of the pilot projects conducted under this section and their possible application nationwide.October 1, 2015
